title: Episode
description: An episodic memory record representing an event experienced or observed by a PeasantFamily agent. Stored in the PostgreSQL 'episodes' table with optional vector embedding for semantic recall.

id.description: UUID primary key, generated by the database (uuid_generate_v4()). Omit when creating; populated on return.

agent_id.description: BESA alias of the agent that owns this episode. Format: '{mode}PeasantFamily{N}' (e.g. 'singlePeasantFamily1').

sim_time.description: Simulation tick (day count) when the episode occurred.

real_time.description: Wall-clock timestamp of insertion. Set by the database DEFAULT. Omit when creating.

text.description: Natural language description of the episode in Spanish. Used for embedding and LLM context. Example: 'Vendí ñame en el mercado a precio bajo. Obtuve $850.000.'.

embedding.description: 384-dimensional float vector from paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2. Null until Stage 3 (EmbeddingService) is activated.

tags.description: Semantic tags for fast filtering without vector search. Use standard vocabulary where possible.

source.description: How this episode entered the agent's memory. 'self' = direct experience; 'rumor' = heard from another agent via social stream; 'observation' = witnessed without direct participation.

metadata.description: Structured data supplementing the text. Domain-specific key-value pairs for analytics and contextual recall.
